title: Infectious Isolation Intake Routing
description: An acute-care operations workflow that coordinates urgent intake, prioritization, and constrained resource assignment under clinical safety rules. Focus case: infectious isolation intake routing.
assumptions: Isolation rooms are single-patient occupancy and require environmental turnover before reuse.
PPE kits and clinical supplies are tracked inventory with finite availability and must be reserved/released.
Laboratory services accept specimens through defined routing and may have variable turnaround times.
Staff assignments are validated against availability before final team allocation.
risks: Insufficient isolation room capacity during surges leading to delayed placement or cohorting.
PPE shortages or misallocation increasing exposure risk to staff and other patients.
Diagnostic delays (lab turnaround) prolonging isolation or causing inappropriate cohorting.
Mis-triage or errors in handover causing incorrect room or team assignment and potential cross-contamination.
